content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Addressed both pending amendments on top of the requirements-timeout fix. The `BackendRole -> BackendPolicyRole` conversion is now kept local to `src/contexts/requirements_drafting/service.rs` via a requirements-specific helper instead of a reusable `BackendRole::to_policy_role()` method, which avoids conflating requirements `CompletionJudge` work with final-review `FinalReviewer` timeout semantics. `AgentExecutionService` now exposes `timeout_for_policy_role(backend_family, policy_role)` and requirements invocations use the resolved target family plus the local role mapping to obtain configured timeouts. I also updated the remaining requirements-service test builders in `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs` and `src/contexts/conformance_spec/scenarios.rs` to pass `EffectiveConfig` into `AgentExecutionService::with_effective_config(...)`, so their requirements paths honor backend family and per-role timeout overrides the same way production does.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Inspect the pending amendment findings against the current timeout fix to confirm whether the global `BackendRole` mapping and non-production requirements builders still needed follow-up changes.
2. [Completed] Remove the misleading global `BackendRole::to_policy_role()` helper, keep the requirements-only `BackendRole -> BackendPolicyRole` mapping local to `src/contexts/requirements_drafting/service.rs`, and switch `AgentExecutionService` to a narrower `timeout_for_policy_role(...)` helper so final-review timeout semantics stay stage-aware elsewhere.
3. [Completed] Update the remaining requirements-service helper builders in `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs` and `src/contexts/conformance_spec/scenarios.rs` to attach the same `EffectiveConfig` clone through `AgentExecutionService::with_effective_config(...)` before constructing `RequirementsService`.
4. [Completed] Re-run validation to confirm formatting, linting, targeted requirements timeout coverage, removal of the hardcoded primary timeout from requirements invocations, the `nix build` gate, and the current repository-wide cargo-test status.

## Validation Evidence

- `nix develop -c cargo fmt --check` passed.
- `nix develop -c cargo clippy --locked -- -D warnings` passed.
- `nix develop -c cargo test --locked --features test-stub quick_mode_uses_configured_backend_timeouts_for_stage_invocations` passed, verifying requirements draft/review/project-seed invocations use configured per-role timeouts.
- `rg -n "Duration::from_secs\(300\)|timeout:\s*std::time::Duration::from_secs|timeout:\s*Duration::from_secs" src/contexts/requirements_drafting/service.rs` returned no matches, confirming the hardcoded primary timeout literal is gone from the requirements service.
- `nix build` passed on the final tree.
- `nix develop -c cargo test --locked --features test-stub` failed with one unrelated repository-wide failure: `workflow_engine_test::pre_commit_failure_remediation_survives_resume` in `tests/unit/workflow_engine_test.rs` (`ResumeFailed { reason: "stage review failed: remediation exhausted at cycle 4; maximum supported cycles is 3" }`).

## Outstanding Risks

- `nix develop -c cargo test --locked --features test-stub` still fails on an existing unrelated test outside this change: `workflow_engine_test::pre_commit_failure_remediation_survives_resume` in `tests/unit/workflow_engine_test.rs`, which panics with `Err(ResumeFailed { reason: "stage review failed: remediation exhausted at cycle 4; maximum supported cycles is 3" })`. The timeout fix and the amendment follow-ups are covered by targeted tests, `cargo fmt`, `clippy`, and `nix build`, but the repository-wide cargo-test acceptance gate is not yet green.
